title: Influence of Quantum Decoherence on the Survival of Neutrino Oscillation Quantumness
authors: Jilali Loulijat, Abdallah Slaoui, Mohamed Gouighri, Berihu Teklu
abstract: This study examines the dynamics of quantumness in two-flavor neutrino oscillations subjected to a dephasing channel, using representative oscillation parameters associated with the KamLAND, MINOS, and Daya Bay experiments. We analyze three complementary quantum-correlation measures-entanglement of formation (EOF), quantum discord (QD), and local quantum uncertainty (LQU)-within an effective two-qubit description. In the unitary case, all three measures display oscillatory behavior controlled by flavor mixing, and their amplitudes are strongly shaped by the relevant mixing angle. MINOS exhibits the largest correlations because $θ_{23}$ is close to maximal, KamLAND shows intermediate values associated with the solar sector, and Daya Bay yields smaller correlations due to the relatively small value of $θ_{13}$. Under dephasing, the off-diagonal coherence terms are suppressed and the three quantifiers decrease accordingly, while QD remains non-zero in regimes where entanglement is weak. For pure states, LQU satisfies $\mathcal{U}=\mathcal{C}^2$ and therefore tracks entanglement monotonically, whereas QD provides a broader witness of non-classical correlations. These results provide a compact quantum-information description of two-flavor neutrino oscillations in both coherent and dephased regimes. We also quantify the sensitivity of these observables to oscillation and decoherence parameters, showing that their main added value relative to flavor probabilities is their direct response to off-diagonal coherence loss.
summary: This paper applies quantum information metrics to study how neutrino flavor oscillations lose their 'quantumness' due to environmental dephasing. It demonstrates that quantum discord can persist even when entanglement vanishes and shows that mixing angles are the primary drivers of correlation strength. The study uses real-world experimental data from KamLAND, MINOS, and Daya Bay to benchmark these effects.
main_problem: The study investigates how environmental dephasing affects the survival and dynamics of quantum correlations (entanglement, discord, and local quantum uncertainty) in neutrino oscillations.
main_result: The researchers find that while dephasing suppresses all quantumness measures, quantum discord remains more robust than entanglement, and the strength of these correlations is primarily driven by mixing angles rather than baseline length.
method: The authors use an effective two-qubit description of two-flavor neutrino oscillations and apply a Lindblad-based dephasing channel to quantify the decay of quantum correlations.
model_or_system: A two-flavor neutrino oscillation model treated as a bipartite two-qubit system, subjected to a phase-damping (dephasing) channel using Kraus operators and the Lindblad master equation.
key_observables: Entanglement of Formation (EOF), Quantum Discord (QD), Local Quantum Uncertainty (LQU), and neutrino survival/transition probabilities.
important_parameters: Mixing angles (theta_12, theta_13, theta_23), mass-squared differences, baseline distances (L), neutrino energy (E), and the Lindblad damping parameter (gamma).
main_assumptions: The study is restricted to a two-flavor oscillation framework and assumes a specific phase-damping model with energy-independent dephasing benchmarks.
figures_summary: Figures show the baseline dependence of EOF, QD, and LQU for the KamLAND, MINOS, and Daya Bay experiments under different fractions of the experimental dephasing bound.
paper_structure: The paper begins by mapping neutrino oscillations to a two-qubit system, defines quantum correlation measures, applies a dephasing model, benchmarks the results against specific neutrino experiments, and concludes with a sensitivity analysis.
why_interesting: It provides a concrete application of open quantum system dynamics and quantum information theory (discord and LQU) to a high-energy particle physics system, demonstrating how non-classical correlations serve as diagnostics for decoherence.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Plots of Local Quantum Uncertainty, Quantum Discord, and Entanglement of Formation as functions of the traveled distance L in km for three experiments: (a) KamLAND, (b) MINOS, and (c) Daya Bay. The ultra-relativistic relation t ≃L is used only to translate the oscillation phase into a baseline variable.

FIG. 2. Baseline dependence of the entanglement of formation un- der the experimentally constrained dephasing benchmark of Eq. (92), with r = Γ/Γ90 = 0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, in (a) KamLAND, (b) MINOS, and (c) Daya Bay.

FIG. 3. Baseline dependence of quantum discord Q(ρAB) under the experimentally constrained dephasing benchmark of Eq. (92), with r = Γ/Γ90 = 0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, in (a) KamLAND, (b) MINOS, and (c) Daya Bay.
FIG. 4. Baseline dependence of LQU U(ρAB) under the experi- mentally constrained dephasing benchmark of Eq. (92), with r = Γ/Γ90 = 0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, in (a) KamLAND, (b) MINOS, and (c) Daya Bay.
